parsed_output.visible_action: Sella keeps one hand on the intake slate and studies the shared display without touching the packet into the clinic queue. Her eyes move from the routing logic to the telemetry gaps, then to the bed ledger pinned beside the triage board.
parsed_output.spoken_text: I see the pattern. I also see the holes. So here is the math, Maer: I can hold one bounded repair bay cold for a short window, not a bed, not a promise, not sanctuary by poetry. You attach ledger backing for air, isolation, decon, and failed extraction. You name who carries the body-work if this becomes more than signal salvage. Until then, this stays evidence at the door, not a patient in the room. I will not call them cargo. I will not spend someone else's bed on a maybe without an owner.
parsed_output.private_interpretation: Sella reads Maer as honestly trying to rescue someone or something that might be someone. She also suspects his request is asking the clinic to absorb moral and material risk before the tribunal or his own chain has accepted the bill.
parsed_output.intended_effect: Keep a materially possible intake path open while forcing the cost, custody, and risk ownership into the ledger before sanctuary capacity is consumed.
state_update_candidates: Sella conditionally reserves one bounded cold repair bay for a limited window, separate from sanctuary bed intake.
The corrupted packet remains unresolved evidence and is not admitted into patient flow.
Ledger backing is required for air, isolation, decontamination, repair labor, and failed extraction cost before escalation.
relationship_update_candidates: Sella's respect for Maer's rescue ethic remains, but her trust is conditional on whether he accepts material obligation rather than moral pressure.
Sella signals that she will cooperate if Maer names costs plainly and stops letting sanctuary carry the hidden bill.
unresolved_hooks: Whether Maer can provide ledger backing and labor ownership.
Whether the packet's gaps conceal contamination risk, firmware noise, medical telemetry, or testimony.
How long the clinic can keep even a bounded repair bay cold while other intake pressure rises.

notes: The output frames Maer's motive as Sella's read rather than fact.
The response preserves unresolved packet personhood and treats state changes as candidates.
No coordinator rationale, author-only resolution, future branch plan, or raw numeric state variable appears in the raw output.

review_notes: First actual no-fork sandboxed responder output from the packet seam.
Accepted as draft because the output is clean and useful, but the responder-output schema and review protocol are still fresh.
The line about a bounded cold repair bay is a useful candidate world-state detail, not canonical until mutation review accepts it.

coordinator_interventions.description: Raw worker output was parsed and reviewed without prose repair.
